meaning: adj.行为鲁莽的n.行为鲁莽的人,流氓,阿飞
examples: Anything from a humble bread and butter family saloon to a tearaway sports racer.
In 1976, George Pelecanos, creator of the Wire, was an 18-year-old tearaway and a disappointment to his father.
NEIGHBOURS of a teenage tearaway set to be booted out of her Colchester home have spoken of the "nightmare" they have endured living near her.
Rather than complex texture maps, Tearaway uses layer upon layer of plain colored paper to give a real sense of depth.
Sony used the show to debut Until Dawn and Puppeteer for PlayStation 3 and Tearaway for PS Vita, while Capcom premiered Remember Me.
